items.q: According to the inverted-U hypothesis, when is performance best?
items.correct: At an optimal moderate level of arousal.
items.why: Students assume 'more psyched up = better'. The inverted-U shows performance rises with arousal up to an optimum, then DECLINES with excess arousal (the athlete chokes). The optimum varies by skill.

items.q: What does the 'I' in FITT stand for?
items.why: Students guess. FITT = Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type - the variables you adjust to apply progressive overload. Intensity = how hard (e.g., % of 1RM or heart rate).
Students confuse storage with circulation. Blood carries glucose; muscles and liver store glycogen (the storage form). Liver glycogen regulates blood sugar; muscle glycogen fuels local contraction.
It's 1896. “1776” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: 1863. “1840” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year of the first modern Olympic Games?
items.why: The real figure is 1896. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
